title: Postgraduate Admissions Process and Eligibility Overview
text:
It is easy to make an application for a master's postgraduate programme by following our advice. Our postgraduate admissions process. Our programmes are suited for those who are completing their first degree and for professionals carrying out work experience. Students appearing for final year bachelor’s examinations are eligible to apply.

Acceptable results: At the postgraduate level, we accept results from various Indian higher education institutions.

Entrance tests: We do not conduct a standardised entrance test or require that applicants appear for an entrance test.

Working professionals: If the applicant is a working professional, we request details of work experience and a resume.
summary: This section outlines the postgraduate admissions process, eligibility criteria for recent graduates and working professionals, acceptable Indian higher education results, and confirms no standardized entrance tests are required.

title: Important Advice for Postgraduate Applications
text:
Important admissions advice:

Applicants can use predicted scores to support an application to study until final grades are released by the relevant higher education institution. Applicants should send final certificates or transcripts as soon as they are available. We cannot enrol new students using their predicted scores.

Applicants must include a personal statement when they make an application to study. Applicants should upload a letter of recommendation from their current higher education institution, college or university. Alternatively, they may provide a letter of reference from their current employer. Applicants should make sure that contact information for any referees is up to date and correct. We may need to get in touch with a referee when we consider an application to study.
summary: This advice covers using predicted scores, the need for final certificates, the mandatory personal statement, and requirements for letters of recommendation from educational institutions or employers.

title: Postgraduate Tuition Fee Refund Policy
text:
Refund policy:

Applicants are expected to pay a deposit of 10% of the tuition fee to secure a place. The first of three instalments of tuition fees is payable by September 8, 2025, the start of the academic term. For any amount paid prior to September 8, 2025, the Distance Selling regulations, applicable to UK universities, mandate us to compulsorily refund any amount paid within 14 days of the acceptance of an offer in full, without any deductions or processing fees as laid out in the Refund Policy. We never hold back an amount if the student has received a conditional offer and is not in a position to meet the academic conditions specified. Tuition Fee refund policy (pdf document)
summary: The refund policy details the 10% tuition fee deposit, installment deadlines, and a mandatory 14-day full refund period before September 8, 2025, adhering to UK Distance Selling regulations for conditional offers.

title: University of Southampton Delhi Postgraduate Programmes
text:
See our programmes. Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, postgraduates will study for one year. All programmes start in August, 2026.

Search programmes:

*   **BEng (Hons) Software Engineering**: Learn to develop reliable, complex and secure software systems. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.
*   **BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance**: A prestigious degree for those aiming to enter the fields of commerce, accountancy and finance. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.
*   **BSc (Hons) Business Management**: Learn to think differently about business and inspire change within organisations and society with an interdisciplinary approach. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.
*   **BSc (Hons) Computer Science**: Learn how to design and build software programs and systems, and find out about the latest advances. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.
*   **BSc (Hons) Creative Computing**: Build immersive and engaging digital experiences that bring art, and technology together. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.
*   **BSc (Hons) Economics**: Learn how our economic decisions affect others, and how business and financial institutions' choices can affect our society. Duration: 3 years. Course Type: Full Time. Start Date: Aug, 2026.

View all programmes.
summary: University of Southampton Delhi offers various one-year postgraduate programmes, equivalent to UK awarded degrees, taught in English. Listed programmes include BEng Software Engineering and BSc degrees in Accounting, Business Management, Computer Science, Creative Computing, and Economics, all starting August 2026.

title: Required Supporting Documents for Postgraduate Application
text:
Supporting documents:

Applicants will need to support their application to study with a range of simple documents. We suggest that applicants have digital copies of these documents easily available to upload before they start to fill in an application to study.

*   **Bachelor’s degree**: A final, or provisional degree, may be submitted as part of the application to study.
*   **Academic transcripts**: For all years or semesters as applicable.
*   **Higher secondary certificate**: Or an equivalent to this.
*   **Proof of identity**: An Aadhar Card or passport is a suitable proof of identity for an application to study.
*   **Letter of recommendation**: This must be on a letterhead from the applicant's educational institution or employer, signed and stamped.
*   **Updated resume**: Showing complete details of an applicant's work experience.
*   **English Language proficiency results (optional)**: Valid proof of IELTS, TOEFL, PTE or Duolingo results in case we cannot offer an English language waiver.

Applications are open now for postgraduate students to walk through the doors in August. Follow this link to start an application today.
summary: Applicants for postgraduate study must provide digital copies of a Bachelor’s degree, academic transcripts, higher secondary certificate, proof of identity (Aadhar Card/passport), a letter of recommendation, an updated resume, and optionally English language proficiency results (IELTS, TOEFL, PTE, Duolingo).

title: Postgraduate Application Frequently Asked Questions
text:
You might also want to know:

*   **What documents do I need to apply for a Postgraduate Master's programme?**
    Academic Certificates (Certificates from previous study), Predicted Achievement (if still completing final year), Academic Transcripts, Proof of English Language proficiency (waiver possible if degree taught in English), Personal Statement (250-500 words), Letter of Reference, Photo-Identity Card (Adhaar card/passport), Resume or Curriculum Vitae (CV).

*   **Should I wait to make my application to study?**
    Students are encouraged to apply as soon as possible for an early Conditional Offer and the best chance to meet conditions, as high demand may close applications earlier.

*   **How much does an application to study cost?**
    There is normally a 2,000 INR application assessment fee for those starting in August 2026.

*   **How can I meet your team?**
    Meet online or in major Indian cities by visiting the Events area of our website to book physical events or register for online sessions. Campus tours can also be booked from the events page.
summary: This FAQ section addresses required documents for a Postgraduate Master's programme, advises applying early due to potential demand, states a 2,000 INR application assessment fee, and explains how to meet the University of Southampton Delhi team online or at events.
